campaigns.name: Pilot to Production
campaigns.objective: Position Danox as the specialist who bridges AI proof-of-concept to scalable production systems
campaigns.targetAudience: Founders and operators at early-stage to Series A companies who have built AI pilots but struggle to scale them
campaigns.positioning: The only consultant who focuses on scaling AI beyond the pilot phase, with proven frameworks for production deployment
keyMessages: Most AI initiatives stall after the pilot phase. Here's how we bridge the gap.
We don't build more prototypes. We build production-ready systems.
AI amplifies your judgment. It doesn't replace it.
Integration over adoption: Enterprise capabilities without enterprise complexity
contentThemes: Case studies showing pilot-to-production success rates and common failure points
Technical deep dives on integration patterns, data governance, and production deployment
Founder stories about scaling AI without drowning in technical debt
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture with pilot-to-production problem framing
Phase 2: Consideration — nurture with technical depth, case studies, and integration frameworks
Phase 3: Conversion — demo requests and SQL handoff for scaling assessments
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — establish presence and top-funnel capture
successMetrics: Engagement rate on pilot-to-production themed posts
Profile visits from target audience segment

campaigns.name: Governance Without Bureaucracy
campaigns.objective: Build authority on AI security and governance frameworks that enable rather than block innovation
campaigns.targetAudience: Marketing leaders and operators at growth-stage companies navigating AI security compliance without slowing velocity
campaigns.positioning: The governance framework that keeps security checkpoints within automated systems, not as roadblocks
keyMessages: Built-in security checkpoints within automated systems, not as roadblocks.
Governance accelerates innovation when designed right.
contentThemes: Practical security checkpoint patterns for automated workflows
Compliance frameworks that integrate with existing infrastructure
Real-world governance adoption rates and security incident reduction
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — problem frame governance as blocker vs. enabler
Phase 2: Consideration — frameworks, adoption metrics, and integration patterns
Phase 3: Conversion — governance assessments and security audits
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — problem frame governance as blocker vs. enabler
successMetrics: Profile visits from security/compliance focused audience

campaigns.name: Human Judgment in the AI Era
campaigns.objective: Differentiate through emphasis on human skills that AI cannot replicate
campaigns.targetAudience: Founders and operators evaluating AI consulting who fear AI will commoditize their expertise
campaigns.positioning: The consultant who leverages AI to amplify human judgment, not replace it
keyMessages: AI amplifies your judgment. It doesn't replace it.
Your human skills are the differentiator in an AI-saturated market
Context, empathy, and strategic synthesis are irreplaceable human skills
contentThemes: Decision quality comparisons: human vs. AI vs. human-AI collaboration
Founder stories on maintaining human oversight in AI workflows
Practical examples of human judgment in AI contexts
phases: Phase 1: Awareness — fear framing around AI commoditization
Phase 2: Consideration — evidence of human-AI collaboration value
campaigns.currentPhase: Phase 1: Awareness — fear framing around AI commoditization
successMetrics: Engagement rate on human-AI collaboration content
Profile visits from skeptical AI adopters

infrastructure.funnelStrategy: Multi-touch LinkedIn-first approach where awareness content drives profile visits, consideration content drives assessment requests, and conversion paths route high-intent prospects to booking calls or assessments
conversionPaths: Primary: Profile → Content engagement → DM or comment → scheduling link → consultation call
Secondary: Content download → automated email nurture → landing page → assessment request form
infrastructure.nurtureApproach: LinkedIn-native documentation (carousels, PDFs) as primary consideration assets, supplemented by weekly email summaries that reinforce key insights and introduce deeper frameworks

scoringSignals: Downloaded ROI calculator or governance framework
Attended webinar or requested case study
Multiple page views on demo content
leadQualification.qualificationThreshold: 60+ points triggers 24-hour outreach; 80+ points triggers 1-hour response
leadQualification.routingGuidance: Route SQL leads (60+) to consultation calendar; hot leads (80+) to immediate call queue with context packet

salesHandoff.process: Automated scoring → notification → context packet assembly → outreach within SLA
requiredContext: Contact info (name, email, phone, authority level)
Engagement history (content downloaded, pages viewed, interaction types)

measurementPlan: Engagement rate on campaign-specific content (target: >5.19% industry median)
Profile visits from target audience segment
Consulting engagement revenue attributed to brand efforts
